summary: Europe's youngest country, a majority-Albanian state that declared independence from Serbia in 2008 — recognised by the US and most of the EU but blocked from the UN by Serbia, Russia, and China.

history.detail: Site of the medieval Serbian-Ottoman battle central to Serb identity.
Milošević strips Kosovo's autonomy, fuelling Albanian resistance.
Serb forces and the KLA fight; mass displacement of Albanians.
NATO bombing forces Serb withdrawal; UN administration begins.
Kosovo unilaterally declares statehood from Serbia.
EU-brokered deal to normalise Serbia-Kosovo relations, only partly implemented.

finance.currency_role: Uses the euro unilaterally (not a eurozone member)
finance.central_bank: Central Bank of the Republic of Kosovo

tech_startups.vc_ecosystem: Early-stage; IT outsourcing and a young workforce

resources.rare_earths: Large lignite reserves; lead, zinc, nickel at Trepca
resources.water: Limited; energy and water infrastructure strained

follow_the_money.detail: Transfers from Kosovars in Germany and Switzerland are a top income source
Historic lead-zinc complex symbolic of the economy and Serb-Albanian dispute
Western aid underpins state institutions and infrastructure

theories_disputed.detail: Recognised by ~100 states and backed by an ICJ opinion; Serbia, Russia and China reject it.
A Council of Europe report alleged wartime crimes by KLA figures; a special court is trying some leaders.

connections.detail: Refuses recognition; tensions flare in the Serb-majority north
Shared Albanian identity and deep cooperation
Key backer; hosts Camp Bondsteel, a major US base
